fields.panel1_caption: emailing yourself files before you quit
fields.panel2_caption: exploiting a post-departure security bug to grab schematics
fields.panel3_caption: texting 'LOL so funny' about it immediately after
fields.panel4_caption: asking job candidates to bring 'actual parts' to your interview as show-and-tell

narrative_summary: The joke escalates through the increasingly brazen alleged trade-secret theft methods in the Apple v. OpenAI lawsuit, landing on Tang Tan's reported 'bring actual hardware to your interview' gambit as the galaxy-brain absurdist peak.
rationale: Candidate 4 is the only entry that actually earns its format — the Expanding Brain template demands escalation, and the four-panel structure delivers it perfectly, moving from mundane (emailing yourself files) to brazen (exploiting a security bug) to self-incriminating (the 'LOL so funny' text) to galaxy-brained absurdist peak (asking interview candidates to bring stolen hardware as show-and-tell). The other four candidates are functionally identical single-beat jokes that use the Disaster Girl template as a caption frame rather than exploiting any visual or structural comedy — the 'LOL so funny' quote is funny once, but it's not a punchline, it's just a restatement of the setup.
story_title: Apple sues OpenAI, accuses ex-employees of stealing trade secrets
place: in a parking garage during an alien invasion
hat_pair: foam speech bubble hat with the word 'typing...' displayed on it
